title: Route Scout config
description: Configuration for route-scout (routescout.config.json). Shared by the CLI and the VSCode extension.

$schema.description: URL of this JSON schema (for editor autocompletion).

root.description: Base directory all globs resolve against. Defaults to the current working directory (ignored by the VSCode extension, which uses the workspace folder).

specs.description: Globs selecting the OpenAPI spec files.

sources.description: Globs selecting the source files scanned for endpoint usage.

exclude.description: Globs excluded from both spec and source discovery.

usage.description: How an endpoint appears in source. Each matcher's `template` is expanded per operation. Placeholders: {operationId}, {OperationId}, {operationId:camel|pascal|kebab|snake|constant}, {method}, {METHOD}, {path}, {pathRegex}.

kind.description: 'symbol' matches a whole identifier (fast; best for generated clients). 'regex' matches a regular expression against each line.

template.description: Template expanded per operation with placeholders.

ignoreImports.description: Mask import / export-from statements (multi-line aware) before matching, so a symbol that only appears in an import never counts as usage.

ignoreLines.description: Extra escape hatch: source lines matching any of these regexes are skipped. Imports are handled by ignoreImports.

importAware.description: Only count a `symbol` match if the identifier was actually imported into the file. Kills false positives from locally-defined / destructured names that share an operationId.

importFrom.description: With importAware, only count imports whose module path contains one of these substrings (e.g. ["generated", "-client"]). Empty means any source.
